name: BRUNNER GLAND HAMARTOMA

demographics.Prevalence: 1.2% of all gastric polyps; 5% of all duodenal masses
demographics.Etiology: response to gastric acid in duodenum → glands protect duodenal epithelium +
demographics.Histo: diffusely enlarged hyperplastic glands of Swiss cheese appearance and variable

dominant_finding: hypoattenuating relative to pancreas (portal venous phase)
discriminating_features: multiple nodular filling defects (usually limited to 1st portion of duodenum) with
heterogeneous echogenicity with various amounts of solid + cystic components
hypoattenuating relative to pancreas (portal venous phase)

general: multiple nodular filling defects (usually limited to 1st portion of duodenum) with
heterogeneous echogenicity with various amounts of solid + cystic components
CECT: hypoattenuating relative to pancreas (portal venous phase)

clinical.Cx: GI bleeding (chronic melena, hematemesis), intestinal obstruction, intussusception
